2606.26783.title: Reproducibility Study of "AlphaEdit: Null-Space Constrained Knowledge Editing for Language Models"
2606.26783.paper_summary: Fang et al. (2025) introduced a null-space constrained projection, named AlphaEdit, for locate-then-edit knowledge editing methods, theoretically guaranteeing that edits do not disrupt previously preserved knowledge, and reports substantial gains over existing editing methods on LLaMA3, GPT2-XL, and GPT-J. In this work, we present a reproducibility study of AlphaEdit, reproducing its reported results under the original experimental setup and extending the evaluation along three axes: new model architectures, additional downstream benchmarks, and substantially longer sequential editing horizons. We successfully reproduce AlphaEdit's reported metrics across the original models, though we identify a discrepancy in the reported fluency and consistency metric. Extending AlphaEdit to newer model families, we find that its advantage does not generalize uniformly, which we trace to architectural assumptions in the locate-then-edit paradigm that are violated by these newer models. We further stress-test AlphaEdit's central sequential-editing claim by extending the number of edits well beyond those evaluated in the original paper, and find that performance, which is stable at the originally reported scale, degrades as edits reach a much higher count, indicating that the null-space projection's protection against catastrophic forgetting is bounded rather than unconditional. Finally, we extend evaluation of edited models on three extra benchmarks, namely, BoolQ, HellaSwag, and XSTest, and we find that large-scale sequential editing degrades both general downstream task competence and safety-relevant refusal behavior. Our results confirm that AlphaEdit performs as reported within its original scope, while showing that its core theoretical guarantees are sensitive to model architecture and editing scale in ways that have practical implications for its deployment.
2606.26783.paper_summary_zh: Fang 等人（2025）提出了一種名為 AlphaEdit 的零空間約束投影，用於定位後編輯的知識編輯方法，理論上保證編輯不會破壞先前保留的知識，並報告在 LLaMA3、GPT2-XL 和 GPT-J 上相較於現有編輯方法有顯著的增益。在本研究中，我們對 AlphaEdit 進行了可重現性研究，重現了其在原始實驗設置下報告的結果，並沿著三個方向擴展了評估：新的模型架構、額外的下游基準和顯著更長的序列編輯範圍。我們成功地重現了 AlphaEdit 在原始模型上的報告指標，儘管我們發現報告的流暢性和一致性指標存在不一致之處。將 AlphaEdit 擴展到更新的模型系列時，我們發現其優勢並未均勻地泛化，這一點我們追溯到定位後編輯範式中的架構假設，這些假設被這些更新的模型所違反。我們進一步對 AlphaEdit 的核心序列編輯主張進行壓力測試，通過將編輯次數擴展到遠超原始論文中評估的次數，發現性能在原始報告的規模下是穩定的，但隨著編輯次數達到更高的數量時，性能會下降，這表明零空間投影對於災難性遺忘的保護是有界而非無條件的。最後，我們在三個額外基準上擴展了對編輯模型的評估，即 BoolQ、HellaSwag 和 XSTest，並發現大規模的序列編輯會降低一般下游任務的能力和安全相關的拒絕行為。我們的結果確認 AlphaEdit 在其原始範疇內的表現如報告所述，同時顯示其核心理論保證對模型架構和編輯規模的敏感性，這對其部署具有實際影響。
2606.26783.author: Ananth K S et.al.
